name: Chef Freddy's White Spaghetti
recipeIngredient: 1 can (4 oz) mushrooms [sliced] or 1 cup fresh mushrooms
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium to large bowl, make a smooth sauce by whisking the flour into the melted butter gradually until the butter no longer absorbs any more flour and you have a thick paste. Whisk in the room temperature milk gradually until it is silky smooth and free of lumps.
2. Pour this mixture into an electric frying pan and simmer on low heat just to keep warm, stirring occasionally to prevent lumping. (You may add more milk a little at a time if needed to keep the consistency, but not thin.)
3. Add the remaining ingredients (except the pasta), whisking completely after each addition, adjusting for your personal taste.
4. Prepare your pasta—fresh is best if you can find it or make it—and stir occasionally as it cooks.
5. When the pasta is tender but not overcooked, drain it in a colander and place it on a warmed serving plate or platter. Carefully pour the sauce over the top and garnish with sprigs of fresh parsley. Serve with fresh Italian bread or rolls (plain or garlic) and a nice Italian wine.
Variations:
a) With meat: Brown 1½ pounds ground beef and 2 tablespoons dried minced onions in a frying pan. Drain off the fat, then pour over the pasta before pouring on the sauce.
b) With meatballs: Prepare your favorite meatball recipe and place them in the sauce when they are done, serving over the pasta.
c) All together now: After the pasta is tender but not overcooked, add the ground beef, stirring to mix well, then add the sauce, again stirring to mix well.
